steps.title: Identify the multiplier for a percentage increase
steps.content: A quantity that is 60% greater than the original equals 100% of the original plus 60%. This is 160% of the original amount, which we express as a multiplier: 1.60. This single multiplication is faster than computing the increase and adding it separately.

steps.title: Multiply the original number of plants by the multiplier
steps.content:
Cedric had 35 plants last year. This year the number is:

$$35 \times 1.60 = 56$$

Cedric has 56 plants in his garden this year.
